trigger: LLM generated async MQTT event loop management using asyncio.get_event_loop() in asyncio task callbacks
symptom: DeprecationWarning on Python 3.10+; breaks silently on 3.12 where get_event_loop() raises RuntimeError when no current event loop
bad_code:
# engine/mqtt_tasks.py
class MQTTEngine:
    async def on_connect(self, client, userdata, flags, rc):
        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()           # line 162
        loop.create_task(self._handle_connect())

    async def on_message(self, client, userdata, msg):
        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()           # line 177
        loop.create_task(self._dispatch(msg))

    async def on_disconnect(self, client, userdata, rc):
        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()           # line 188
        loop.create_task(self._handle_disconnect())
good_code:
# engine/mqtt_tasks.py
class MQTTEngine:
    async def on_connect(self, client, userdata, flags, rc):
        asyncio.ensure_future(self._handle_connect())

    async def on_message(self, client, userdata, msg):
        asyncio.ensure_future(self._dispatch(msg))

    async def on_disconnect(self, client, userdata, rc):
        asyncio.ensure_future(self._handle_disconnect())
notes: asyncio.get_event_loop() deprecated in 3.10, raises RuntimeError in 3.12 when called outside a running loop. Use asyncio.get_running_loop() or asyncio.ensure_future() inside coroutines.
